bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my maximum utility offer. My highest utility values across all issues are: issueA=valueE (1.0), issueB=valueE (1.0), issueC=valueE (1.0), issueD=valueE (1.0), issueE=valueE (1.0). This gives me a utility of 1.0 across all weighted issues. Starting strong is appropriate at the beginning of negotiation with no time pressure yet.

The opponent opened with a very aggressive bid heavily favoring their own interests (low utility values for me across the board). At t=0.02, we're very early in the negotiation, so I should stay close to my top-utility bid. My opening was all valueE (max utility ~1.0). I'll make a very minor concession on the least important issues (issueA and issueC, both weight 0.1) while holding firm on issueE (weight 0.3), issueB and issueD (weight 0.2 each). I'll drop issueA to valueD and issueC to valueD to signal willingness to negotiate while maintaining high overall utility.

The opponent is holding firm on their low-utility offers (issueA: valueA, issueB: valueA, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueA/valueB), showing no concession. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay close to my top utility while making minor concessions on low-weight issues. My highest weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2) — I'll hold firm on those. I can slightly concede on issueA and issueC (both weight 0.1) to signal willingness to negotiate.

The opponent is holding very firm on issueA (valueA), issueB (valueA), issueC (valueB), and issueE (valueA/valueB), with only slight movement on issueD (from valueA to valueC). It's very early (t=0.06), so I should stay close to my top utility while making small concessions. My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2) — I'll hold firm on these. IssueA and issueC have low weight (0.1 each), so I can concede a bit there. I'll keep issueE at valueE, issueB at valueE, issueD at valueE, and try valueB on issueA (lower utility but low weight) while keeping issueC at valueD.

The opponent is consistently offering very low values for me (valueA/valueB across most issues), showing little concession. Their issueD moved from valueA to valueC to valueB - somewhat inconsistent. It's very early (t=0.08), so I should stay firm on my high-weight issues (issueE weight 0.3, issueB and issueD weight 0.2 each). I'll make a small concession on low-weight issues (issueA, issueC) while holding firm on issueE and issueD which matter most to me. I'll try valueB for issueA and valueC for issueC, keeping issueE at valueE, issueD at valueE, and issueB at valueD as a slight concession.

The opponent is largely holding firm on their preferred values (valueA for issueA, issueB, issueD; valueB for issueC; varying for issueE). Their offers give me very low utility. It's still early (t=0.1), so I should stay near my high-utility bids. My most important issue is issueE (0.3 weight) where I want valueE, and issueB and issueD (0.2 each) where I want high values. The opponent seems to care about issueA and issueB being valueA, and issueC being valueB - these are low-weight issues for me, so I can concede on issueA (low weight 0.1) while holding firm on issueE and issueD. I'll try conceding issueA to valueA and issueC to valueB to signal flexibility on low-weight issues while keeping my high-weight issues strong.

The opponent seems to be holding fairly firm on issueA (always valueA), issueC (always valueB), and mostly valueA for issueD, while showing slight movement on issueB (now valueC) and issueE (varying). My highest weight issues are issueE (0.3) and issueB/issueD (0.2 each). I should hold firm on issueE (valueE) and issueD (valueE) since they're most important to me, and concede a bit more on lower-weight issues like issueA and issueC where the opponent seems to care a lot. I'll try offering issueA: valueA, issueC: valueB (matching opponent preferences on low-weight issues) while keeping issueE: valueE, issueD: valueE, and slightly conceding issueB to valueD.

The opponent seems to be holding very firm on issueA (always valueA), issueB (mostly valueA), issueD (mostly valueA), and issueE (mostly low values). My highest-weight issues are issueE (0.3), issueB (0.2), and issueD (0.2). I should hold firm on these high-weight issues while conceding on lower-weight ones. The opponent occasionally moves on issueC and issueD slightly. I'll keep issueE at valueE and issueD at valueE (my top priorities), concede issueA and issueC to values the opponent seems to prefer, and keep issueB high. This is still early (t=0.14), so I can maintain a strong position.

The opponent seems to be mostly anchored around valueA for issueA, issueB, issueD, and alternating on issueC/issueE. Their latest offer has issueE: valueD which is actually decent for me (0.91 utility). I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueE at 0.3, issueB and issueD at 0.2 each) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. At t=0.16 we're still early, so I can maintain a high utility target around 0.85+. I'll keep issueE at valueE (my top priority), keep issueD at valueD, and make minor concessions on issueA and issueC.

The opponent seems to be holding firm on issueA=valueA and issueC=valueB, while occasionally varying issueD and issueE. Their latest offer includes issueD=valueE (which is great for me, utility 1.0) and issueE=valueC (utility 0.61 for me). I should hold firm on issueE (my highest weight issue at 0.3) and issueD (weight 0.2), while being willing to concede on lower-weight issues. At t=0.18, we're still early, so I'll maintain a high utility bid but try to signal some flexibility on issueB and issueC where the opponent seems to care more. I'll keep issueE=valueE and issueD=valueE (my top priorities) while conceding slightly on issueB to valueC.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.20) is: issueA: valueA, issueB: valueC, issueC: valueB, issueD: valueE, issueE: valueB. Let me calculate its utility to me: issueA(0.1*0.20) + issueB(0.2*0.56) + issueC(0.1*0.73) + issueD(0.2*1.00) + issueE(0.3*0.31) = 0.02 + 0.112 + 0.073 + 0.20 + 0.093 = 0.498. The opponent seems to be conceding slightly (issueB moved to valueC, issueD moved to valueE). My current bid gives me high utility on issueE (my most important issue at 0.3 weight). I should hold firm on issueE: valueE and issueD: valueE (both high weight), while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll keep issueE: valueE and issueD: valueE, and try to meet the opponent on issueB by offering valueC (which they seem to accept), and keep issueC: valueB and issueA: valueA.

The opponent seems to be holding firm on issueA=valueA, issueC=valueB, and mostly issueE=valueB/valueC, while occasionally conceding on issueD and issueB. My highest weight issue is issueE (0.3), and I should hold firm there. The opponent's latest offer dropped back on issueD to valueA, which is bad for me. I'll maintain my strong position on issueE=valueE and issueD=valueE (both high weight), while keeping issueA=valueA (low weight, opponent wants it), issueC=valueB (low weight, opponent wants it), and slightly conceding on issueB to valueC which I've been offering. This is still early (t=0.22), so I can hold near my current position.

The opponent seems to be oscillating rather than consistently conceding - they keep returning to valueA for issueB and issueD, and their issueE values vary. My highest-weight issue is issueE (0.3), and I've been holding firm on valueE there. The opponent's last offer gave issueD: valueD which is good for me (0.98 utility). I should maintain my strong position on issueE and issueD while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. At t=0.24, I'm still early-mid game, so I can hold relatively firm. I'll try offering issueE: valueE, issueD: valueE, issueB: valueC, and concede slightly on issueC to valueB, keeping issueA at valueA.

The opponent is consistently anchoring on valueA for issueA and issueD (low utility for me), while I've been holding firm on issueE (valueE, my highest weight issue at 0.3). The opponent's offers give me very low utility (~0.3-0.4). At t=0.26, we're still early-mid game, so I should maintain high utility bids. My current best offer gives me: issueA(valueA)=0.02 + issueB(valueC)=0.112 + issueC(valueB)=0.073 + issueD(valueE)=0.2 + issueE(valueE)=0.3 = ~0.705. I'll try a slight concession on issueB (drop to valueC which I've been offering) while keeping issueE at valueE and issueD at valueE. I'll try conceding issueC to valueC to signal flexibility on low-weight issues.

The opponent is still offering very low utility to me - their latest bid (issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueA, issueE: valueB) gives me roughly 0.1*0.33 + 0.2*0.20 + 0.1*0.87 + 0.2*0.42 + 0.3*0.31 ≈ 0.033+0.04+0.087+0.084+0.093 = 0.337. My current best offer gives ~0.87 utility. At t=0.28, we're still early-mid game, so I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueE, issueB, issueD) while making small concessions on low-weight issues. The opponent seems to strongly prefer valueA/valueB on most issues. I'll keep issueE at valueE (highest weight), issueD at valueE, issueB at valueC, and concede slightly on issueC to valueB, issueA to valueA.

The opponent has been mostly holding firm on low-value offers for me, but I can see some movement - they've occasionally offered issueD: valueE (t=0.18) and issueD: valueC/valueD at times. My highest priority is issueE (0.3 weight) where I want valueE, and issueB/issueD (0.2 each). The opponent seems to prefer low values on issueA and issueB, but has shown some flexibility on issueD and issueC. At t=0.3, I'm still early-mid game, so I should maintain high utility on my key issues (issueE, issueD) while making small concessions on lower-weight issues. I'll keep issueE: valueE and issueD: valueE (my top priorities), concede slightly on issueB to valueB, and keep issueC: valueB which the opponent seems to accept.
